reason: The score and justifications are well-supported by fan/critic consensus. The key claims all check out: (1) the tongue-lick is confirmed improv by Tennant with no book basis, correctly identified; (2) the book trial scene showing a pleading, desperate Barty vs. the film's defiant/arrogant portrayal is a widely-cited difference - Quora and ScreenRant both confirm the book version pleads innocence and begs his father while the film gives him an arrogant, confident demeanor; (3) the film omitting the full Veritaserum confession scene (which would have conveyed motivational complexity) is documented by ScreenRant; (4) the Dementor's kiss being cut is a real omission that removes his final arc resolution. The score of 64 is reasonable - the core narrative function is preserved (sleeper agent engineering Voldemort's return) but personality and motivational depth are genuinely flattened. The only slight weakness is that the motivations score (9/25) may be slightly harsh - the film does hint at his fanatical devotion even if the internal conflict is absent - but this is a matter of interpretation, not a factual error.
